title: C4 Model Structured Data
description: Contract for codebase/architecture/{system,containers,components,dataflow,entities,statemachine}.json — the codebase → product contract. These files carry C4 model data in a structured form that product skills can read for feasibility review during draft review. Each file describes one level of the C4 zoom.

level.description: Which level of the C4 (or adjacent) zoom this file describes.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this file was produced.

skill.description: The skill that produced this file (e.g., 'archeia:write-tech-docs').

skill_version.description: Skill version for traceability of regenerated content.

elements.description: The set of elements at this C4 level. For system.json, these are external actors and the system boundary. For containers.json, services/databases/queues. For components.json, internal modules. For dataflow.json, flows. For entities.json, ORM/schema entities. For statemachine.json, state machines.

id.description: Stable identifier for this element, referenced by other elements' relationships.

description.description: One-sentence description grounded in the codebase.

technology.description: Technology or framework this element uses (e.g., 'PostgreSQL', 'Redis', 'React').

evidence.description: File paths in the source tree that were cited as evidence for this element's existence and description.

description.description: Nature of the relationship (e.g., 'reads from', 'publishes to', 'invokes').

archeia.evidence_principle: Every element's 'evidence' array must cite at least one file path in the source tree. Elements without evidence must be flagged or omitted — no fabrication.
